evals.prompt: How do I create a DeepReadonly type in TypeScript?
evals.input: nested config object with mutable properties
evals.expected: Recursive mapped type using readonly modifier on nested objects — `type DeepReadonly<T> = { readonly [P in keyof T]: T[P] extends object ? T[P] extends Function ? T[P] : DeepReadonly<T[P]> : T[P] }`

evals.prompt: Implement a type-safe event emitter with TypeScript generics
evals.input: EventMap with user:created { id, name }, user:updated { id } events
evals.expected: TypedEventEmitter<T extends Record<string, any>> class with on<K extends keyof T> and emit<K extends keyof T> methods; callbacks receive T[K] typed data

evals.prompt: Extract the resolved type of an async function
evals.expected: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof fetchUser>> resolves to User; explanation of Awaited<T> unwrapping nested Promises

evals.prompt: Create a type that picks only string properties from an interface
evals.input: interface Mixed { id: number; name: string; age: number; email: string }
evals.expected: PickByType<T, U> using mapped type with key remapping: `type PickByType<T, U> = { [K in keyof T as T[K] extends U ? K : never]: T[K] }` → result: { name: string; email: string }

evals.prompt: How do I use discriminated unions for a state machine?
evals.expected: Union type with literal `type` discriminant; switch(state.type) provides exhaustive narrowing; each branch has correct payload properties typed
